story.title: Dr. Jackson and the Energy Chain
story.text:
Hello, I am Dr. Brianna Jackson. I am an Energy Systems Physicist. I track how energy moves and changes in complex systems.

Think about a roller coaster at the top of a big hill. It has a lot of stored energy because of its height. We call this potential energy. More height or more mass means more stored energy.

As the coaster falls, it goes faster and faster. The stored potential energy transforms into kinetic energy. This is the energy of motion. At the bottom, the coaster has maximum kinetic energy.

But then it slows down. Where does that energy go? The wheels rub the track. This friction generates thermal energy, which is heat. Some energy also becomes sound. The energy transformation continues. The energy is not lost. It just changes form. This is the principle of conservation of energy.

At my lab, I apply this idea. We design systems, like electric car brakes, that capture this energy instead of wasting it as heat. Understanding this energy chain helps us build a more efficient world.
think_abouts: Where does a roller coaster have the most potential energy?
What happens to the kinetic energy when the coaster slows down due to friction?
story.go_deeper: How is the energy transformation in a car crash different from two billiard balls hitting each other?

margin_defs.definition: changes from one form to another

check_prompts: What kind of energy does friction create?

vocabulary.definition: Energy stored in an object due to its position or height — the higher it is, the more energy is stored.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The book on the top shelf has more potential energy than the book on the floor.

vocabulary.definition: Energy of motion — a moving object has kinetic energy based on its mass and speed.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A fast-moving car has a lot of kinetic energy.

vocabulary.definition: Heat energy generated by friction when surfaces rub together.
vocabulary.use_sentence: When you rub your hands together, you create thermal energy.

vocabulary.definition: The principle that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The law of conservation of energy explains why a bouncing ball eventually stops.

vocabulary.definition: The conversion of energy from one form to another, such as potential to kinetic.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A light bulb performs an energy transformation from electrical to light and heat energy.

discussion_cards.prompt: Think of another example of potential energy transforming into kinetic energy. Describe the energy chain.
discussion_cards.frame: In a _____, the potential energy from _____ transforms into kinetic energy when _____.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Jackson works to make systems more efficient. Why is it important to not waste energy as heat?
discussion_cards.frame: It is important to not waste energy because _____.

discussion_cards.prompt: Where in your school day do you see energy transformations happening?
discussion_cards.frame: I see an energy transformation when I _____. The energy changes from _____ to _____.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
